ELPAC Level 1
Lexile 1000–1300L
DTR · TALK ABOUT A SCENE

The Case of the Shared Six

STS-MS6-L1-10001300-MSMATH6-MCF-LAT-01 • MS Math Grade 6 • Greatest Common Factor and Least Common Multiple
Look at the picture. You will answer some questions about it. Press the play button to hear each question. Record your answer for each question. You can listen to your answer and record it again. Click SUBMIT RECORDINGS when you have answered all the questions.
JUST FOR FUN · OPTIONAL
Real detectives look for what two clues have in common. Sofía, Mateo, and Camila are cracking a case about the numbers 12 and 18. They need the biggest number that divides into both to unlock the next clue, and Ms. Reyes just circled it: 6. In the tradition of a mercado puzzle wall, every clue connects to another by the pattern it shares.
The next clue needs the smallest number that both 4 and 6 divide into. What is it?
Optional. You do not have to record this one.
1
What is Sofía doing?
2
What is Mateo holding?
3
What is the teacher doing?
4
What room is this? How do you know?
5
Tell me about the evidence board.
6
What is Camila wearing?
Record an answer for each question, then submit.
